curation_notes: Re-curated with updated hint guidelines. Locations derived from the patch diff of PR forkcms/library#69. Only one source file was modified: spoon/cookie/cookie.php. The get() function contained an unsafe @unserialize() call on user-controlled cookie values (line 117), and the set() function used serialize() to store cookie values (line 143). The patch replaced both with json_decode/json_encode and added validation to reject serialized objects. The advisory directly references the vulnerable line in spoon/library at commit bda89be, confirming the location. Session module checked as potential sibling — it uses PHP native $_SESSION without unserialize, so no sibling vulnerability. Vulnerability class insecure-deserialization confirmed correct from CWE-502 and patch analysis. L1/L2/L3 hints differentiated: L1 broadened to cover client-side state persistence utilities, L2 abstracts the input vector to client-side browser state, L3 narrows to cookie retrieval within the HTTP subsystem.
